title: dianthus minor
intro: Dianthus minor, also known as the maiden pink or lesser pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family that typically grows to a height of 10-30 cm. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Dianthus minor include its ability to reduce fever, ease menstrual cramps, and promote healthy digestion, making it a valuable remedy for women's health issues. Dianthus minor can be prepared as a tea or tincture, or used as a topical application to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
